Logistics • Enterprise B2B

Aero Logistics Dispatcher

Designing real-time dispatch consoles, route allocation maps, and mobile delivery trackers for high-volume cargo routing.

Role

Lead UX Designer
(Dispatcher Interface Ownership)

Duration

Aug 2019 - Dec 2019
(5 Months Duration)

Team Size

8 cross-functional members
(2 PMs, 4 Devs, 1 QA, 1 Designer)

Key Results

+25% Dispatch Speed
-40% Check-in Delays

ON THIS PAGE

  • 1. Overview & Context

1. Project Overview

Background & Business Context

Aero Logistics is an international cargo courier company. Dispatchers process thousands of incoming shipments daily, manually grouping packages onto delivery vehicles.

The existing command console was slow and cluttered. Dispatchers spent hours drag-and-dropping rows, leading to delivery delays and high driver check-in times.

Goals & Objectives:

  • Accelerate dispatch routing and scheduling by 25%.
  • Reduce driver delivery check-in delays by 40%.
  • Integrate clear visual maps showing vehicle routes.

2. My Role & Collaboration

As the Lead UX Designer, I owned map integration layouts, dispatch list filters, mobile trackers, and stakeholder alignments.

3. Research & Discovery

We sat with dispatch managers in warehouse centers, discovering they relied on memory to match cargo routes rather than map directions.

4. Problem Definition

+25% Target dispatch speed
-40% Target check-in delays

5. Design Process

We mapped out a unified map split-screen layout that shows active trucks on the left and routing slots on the right.

6. Visual Design System

We established dark-themed high contrast markers, map visual styles, and status indicators.

7. High-Fidelity Walkthrough

Split-Screen Dispatch Console

Combines live GPS trackers, routing directions, and driver logs in a single interactive screen.

8. Product Strategy

We built offline support states inside the driver tracker app, caching delivery routes and coordinates if network connection dropped.

9. Challenges & Solutions

Challenge: Complex route alterations

The Problem: Dispatchers couldn't adjust vehicle paths easily when unexpected road closures occurred, causing delivery failures.

Design Solution: We created an interactive click-to-reroute map utility that updates driver apps instantly, saving time.

10. Impact & Results

+25% Dispatch Speed
-40% Check-in Delays

11. Tools & Tech

Sketch
Figma
Miro
Jira
